name: Apple Dumplings with Almonds
recipeInstructions:
-----------------------------------DOUGH-----------------------------------
240 grams (8 ounces) flour
40 grams (1 ounce) caster sugar
150 grams (5 ounces) butter, softened
1 egg
---------------------------------SERVE WITH---------------------------------
Lightly whipped cream,
custard or ice cream
To Drink: Sweet white wine or Madeira
Level of Difficulty: Fairly easy Preparation Time: 1 hour + resting time
Prepare in Advance: Yes Line a baking sheet with greaseproof paper. Make the dough by combining all the ingredients in a bowl and working them together into a smooth dough. Set aside in the fridge to rest for at least 1 hour.
Peel and core the apples. Prick the apples with a cocktail stick or needle. Brush with a little of the brandy and roll in the chopped almonds.
Grate the almond paste, add the remaining brandy and mix together until smooth.
Preheat the oven to 200°C/400°F/Gas Mark 6. Roll out the dough to form a square approximately 30 x 30 cm/12 x 12 inches. Cut the square into 4 pieces. Put one apple on each square. Fill the centers with the almond paste mixture. Brush the dough edges with beaten egg. Fold the four corners over the apples and pinch the edges together to form a parcel. Put the apple dumplings on the prepared baking sheet, brush with egg and sprinkle with the remaining chopped almonds. Bake for 20-25 minutes. Test with a fork or skewer to see if the apples are fully cooked; hard apples will take longer. Cover the dumplings with foil if they get too brown during baking.
